experience.heading: EXPERIENCE_
experience.intro: Computer Science student at ITESM with a Cybersecurity concentration, combining fullstack engineering experience with hands-on security lab work.
experience.eduDescription: Instituto Tecnológico y de Estudios Superiores de Monterrey (ITESM), Monterrey, NL. Cybersecurity concentration. Graduating June 2026.
experience.highlight1: Cybersecurity concentration — network security, offensive and defensive operations.
experience.highlight2: Fullstack development — React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, TypeScript.
experience.highlight3: Multiple projects with real clients in collaboration with companies like Neoris, Ikusi/Velatia, and Tec de Monterrey.
experience.volSubtitle: Service Learning — Federación Unida de Ciencia · Monterrey, MX
experience.volDescription: Development of a React web application for employee credential management, connected to an Airtable backend, allowing the HR team to manage records without engineering support.
experience.deliverable1: React app displaying employee credentials (front and back) from an Airtable database.
experience.deliverable2: No-code backend via Airtable — HR team updates records from a spreadsheet interface.
experience.itesmDesc: Graduating June 2026. Cybersecurity concentration. Monterrey, Mexico.

projects.heading: DECRYPTING
projects.description: A repository of full-stack architectures and cybersecurity labs. Each entry represents a real challenge solved with precise engineering and a security-first approach.

tetrapak.title: TetrapakLogistics
tetrapak.description: Full-stack logistics management system for recycling center operations with RBAC for 4 user types. REST API with 15+ endpoints, JWT authentication, and PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM. Interactive maps with geofencing (500m radius) and route optimization (Nearest Neighbor TSP + OSRM).

welcomehub.title: WelcomeHub — Neoris
welcomehub.description: Employee onboarding platform for Neoris with 2 user roles, differentiated dashboards, course management with progress tracking, and a gamified leaderboard. AI chatbot (Compi) integrated with ChatGPT and time-tracking module with admin approval workflow.

awaq.title: AWAQ Gamified Training
awaq.description: Full-stack web dashboard with C# .NET MVC backend and responsive HTML/CSS/JS frontend for NGO volunteer data management. Unity game integration (AWAQ Tower) exported to iPad — cross-platform gamified experience with real-time progress sync.

retailLab.title: Retail Cyber Lab — Ikusi/Velatia
retailLab.description: Comprehensive retail security project for Ikusi/Velatia: 3-phase architecture design + Proxmox lab with 12 VMs (5 network segments). F5 BIG-IP WAF, OPNsense inter-VLAN firewall, Suricata IPS, Security Onion SIEM. Red team offensive simulations (ransomware delivery, reverse shell, keylogger) validated by Suricata blocking rules and Security Onion threat detection.

networkMgmt.title: Network Management — Velatia
networkMgmt.description: Design and configuration of multi-site network infrastructure for a multinational company, with secure and scalable router and switch configurations across multiple locations. Cost-benefit analysis under real enterprise constraints. Velatia diploma for outstanding performance.

caritas.title: Cáritas Monterrey — Secure API
caritas.description: Python REST API deployed on Linux with HTTPS/SSL certification and RBAC separating volunteer, staff, and admin workflows. Encrypted communication between an iOS client (Swift) and an on-campus SQL Server database via SSL certificates.

tetrapak.title: Project Spotlight — TetrapakLogistics
tetrapak.archDesc: Fullstack application with a React frontend and Node.js/Express backend deployed on Vercel. PostgreSQL database managed through Prisma ORM, providing typed migrations and type-safe queries. REST API with 15+ endpoints covering CRUD operations, authentication, and business logic.
tetrapak.archBullet1: Frontend: React with React Router for SPA navigation
tetrapak.archBullet2: Backend: Node.js + Express with 15+ endpoint REST API
tetrapak.archBullet3: Database: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM for typed migrations and queries
tetrapak.archBullet4: Deployment: Vercel with automated CI/CD from GitHub
tetrapak.rbacDesc: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system with 4 differentiated user types. JWT authentication with refresh tokens for secure session management. Each role has granular permissions restricting access to specific endpoints and platform functionalities.
tetrapak.rbacBullet1: 4 roles: Administrator, Route Operator, Community, and Viewer
tetrapak.rbacBullet2: JWT authentication with access + refresh tokens
tetrapak.rbacBullet3: Authorization middleware validating permissions per endpoint
tetrapak.rbacBullet4: Registration and login with data validation and password hashing
tetrapak.mapsDesc: Interactive mapping module with Leaflet for geospatial visualization of recycling centers and collection routes. Geofencing implementation with 500m radius for location validation and route optimization algorithm using Nearest Neighbor TSP with OSRM routing engine for real road distance calculations.
tetrapak.mapsBullet1: Interactive maps with Leaflet — markers, polygons, and custom layers
tetrapak.mapsBullet2: Geofencing with 500m radius for center location validation
tetrapak.mapsBullet3: TSP algorithm (Nearest Neighbor) for visit order optimization
tetrapak.mapsBullet4: OSRM engine for real road distance calculations

welcomehub.title: Project Spotlight — WelcomeHub
welcomehub.youtubeTitle: WelcomeHub — Neoris Employee Onboarding Platform Demo
welcomehub.credit: Demo by Santiago (teammate) · Next.js + TypeScript + Supabase + AI chatbot (ChatGPT)
welcomehub.demoDesc: Full demonstration of the WelcomeHub platform built for Neoris, showcasing the employee onboarding flow, role-differentiated dashboards, course management system with progress tracking, and the integrated AI chatbot.
welcomehub.archDesc: Fullstack application deployed on Vercel with Next.js and TypeScript on the backend, Supabase (PostgreSQL) for database and authentication, and React frontend with Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui. GPT API integration for the Compi chatbot and LangChain for natural language processing.
welcomehub.archBullet1: Frontend: Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui + Framer Motion
welcomehub.archBullet2: Backend: Next.js API Routes with Supabase (PostgreSQL) for persistence
welcomehub.archBullet3: AI: GPT API integrated with LangChain and Transformers for Compi chatbot
welcomehub.archBullet4: CI/CD: Git + GitHub with automatic deployment on Vercel
welcomehub.aiDesc: Compi is an AI assistant integrated with the GPT API that helps new employees during their onboarding process. It answers questions about company policies, courses, and procedures using text generation with credit-limited API keys.
welcomehub.aiBullet1: Direct GPT API integration — configured via API key with credit limits
welcomehub.aiBullet2: Persistent conversation history per user stored in Supabase
welcomehub.aiBullet3: Real-time chat interface with contextual company responses
welcomehub.onboardDesc: Onboarding system with 2 differentiated roles (Admin and Employee). Admins manage courses, users, and time approvals. Employees complete courses with progress tracking and compete on a gamified leaderboard.
welcomehub.onboardBullet1: 2 roles: Admin (full management) and Employee (courses + progress)
welcomehub.onboardBullet2: Course system with states: pending, in progress, completed
welcomehub.onboardBullet3: Gamified leaderboard with ranking by total progress
welcomehub.onboardBullet4: Time tracking module with admin approval workflow

awaq.title: Project Spotlight — AWAQ Gamified Training
awaq.credit: C# .NET MVC Dashboard + Unity game (AWAQ Tower) exported to iPad · NGO volunteer training with real-time progress sync
awaq.demoDesc: Full demonstration of the complete system: web administration dashboard and the AWAQ Tower game running on iPad, showing real-time progress synchronization between both platforms.
awaq.gameDesc: Educational game developed in Unity and exported to iPad for training AWAQ (Biological Stations) NGO volunteers. The game communicates with the backend through UnityWebRequest, sending scores, times, and completed tasks to a custom REST API.
awaq.gameBullet1: Developed in Unity with C# — exported as native iPad build
awaq.gameBullet2: Backend communication via UnityWebRequest to custom REST API
awaq.gameBullet3: Real-time synchronization of scores, times, and task progress
awaq.dashDesc: Fullstack dashboard with C# .NET MVC backend and responsive HTML/CSS/JavaScript frontend. Manages AWAQ volunteer data: employee scores, game times, completed tasks, and overall progress. Administration interface for the NGO.
awaq.dashBullet1: Management of scores, times, and completed tasks per volunteer
awaq.dashBullet2: Statistics panel with overall progress and performance metrics
awaq.dashBullet3: Volunteers listing with AWAQ NGO data
awaq.dashBullet4: C# .NET MVC backend with SQL Server connection for persistence

cyberLab.title: Project Spotlight — Retail Cyber Lab
cyberLab.infraDesc: The infrastructure is deployed on <1>Proxmox VE</1>, hosting <3>12 virtual machines</3> distributed across 5 network segments replicating a real retail environment. VMs include Windows Server (AD, DNS, FTP, DHCP), Linux systems (Ubuntu, Security Onion SIEM), virtualized network appliances (F5 BIG-IP vLTM, OPNsense), and workstations (Kali Linux for attacks, Windows 10 as victims).
cyberLab.infraBullet3: Services: Active Directory, DNS, DHCP, FTP
cyberLab.infraBullet4: Zones: DMZ, Servers, Users, Attack, Management
cyberLab.f5Desc1: <1>F5 BIG-IP</1> is deployed as a load balancing and web application firewall (WAF) platform. Backend nodes, <3>virtual servers</3>, and balancing pools are configured, establishing traffic distribution policies that optimize service performance and availability.
cyberLab.f5Desc2: The <1>WAF</1> module is enabled for deep HTTP/S traffic inspection, detection of common attacks (SQLi, XSS, OWASP Top 10), and application of custom security policies, ensuring robust perimeter protection.
cyberLab.f5Bullet3: Custom policies: SQLi, XSS, OWASP Top 10
cyberLab.opnDesc1: <1>OPNsense</1> acts as the lab's main firewall and router, managing traffic between the 5 network segments. VLAN interfaces, granular firewall rules with ACLs, and routing policies were configured to ensure <3>zone isolation</3>.
cyberLab.opnDesc2: The integrated IDS/IPS module complements the F5 WAF by inspecting internal traffic and generating alerts for known attack patterns between segments.
cyberLab.redDesc: Real attacks were executed from Kali Linux: simulated ransomware delivery, Metasploit reverse shell, and active keylogger on user workstations. The attacks validated the complete compromise chain and forced defensive controls to respond.
cyberLab.blueDesc: Suricata IPS blocked malicious traffic in real-time through custom rules. Security Onion centralized logs and generated correlated alerts in its SIEM dashboard, validating the detection of each Red Team attack.

caritas.title: Project Spotlight — Cáritas Monterrey
caritas.archDesc: Server hosted on-premise at Tec de Monterrey campus with a 3-tier architecture: iOS mobile client (10.22.x.x), Flask REST API (10.14.255.62), and SQL Server database (100.80.80.7). Docker DNS for internal resolution and UFW firewalls at every stack layer.
caritas.archBullet1: UFW restricts port 10206 to Tec mobile subnet only (10.22.0.0/16)
caritas.archBullet2: HTTPS/SSL encrypted communication with TLSv1.2 certificates
caritas.archBullet3: DB server isolated — accepts connections only from API IP
caritas.archBullet4: SSH restricted to internal network (10.0.0.0/8) for administration
caritas.auditDesc: Security audits with Bandit (Python static analysis) and OWASP ZAP (dynamic analysis). SQL Injection vulnerabilities and high-severity issues (debug mode in production) were identified and remediated by migrating to Stored Procedures and disabling debug.
caritas.auditBullet1: Bandit: Issue B201 (High Severity) → remediated to 0 issues
caritas.auditBullet2: OWASP ZAP: SQL Injection detected in dynamic queries
caritas.auditBullet3: Migration from dynamic queries to Stored Procedures (GetUserTotalPoints)
caritas.auditBullet4: Debug disabled in production — ssl_context active
caritas.cryptoDesc: Handling sensitive data for the social partner organization Cáritas Monterrey. Implemented Peppering with SHA-256 for password hashing, credentials managed via environment variables (.env), and Least Privilege principle with a limited DB user (iborrego) instead of SA.
caritas.cryptoBullet2: Least Privilege: 'iborrego' user with limited permissions, not SA
caritas.cryptoBullet3: Zero hardcoded credentials — all via os.getenv()
caritas.cryptoBullet4: RBAC: separate workflows for volunteers, staff, and admins
caritas.riskDesc: Radar chart that mathematically visualizes how the implemented security measures reduced the project's initial attack surface. Each axis represents a threat vector evaluated before and after applying controls.

velatia.title: Project Spotlight — Velatia Network Management
velatia.networkDesc: Complete network infrastructure design for the Paradox company (Velatia case), a multinational electric vehicle corporation headquartered in Monterrey with branches in Guadalajara, Querétaro, Mexico City, and Austin, Texas. Router and switch configuration with Ethernet and wireless connectivity across all sites.
velatia.networkBullet1: 5 interconnected sites: Monterrey (HQ), Guadalajara, Querétaro, CDMX, Austin TX
velatia.networkBullet2: VLAN segmentation to isolate traffic by department and function
velatia.networkBullet3: Router and switch configuration with scalable routing protocols
velatia.networkBullet4: Ethernet and wireless connectivity for devices at each site
velatia.costDesc: Detailed cost evaluation under real Velatia enterprise constraints. Network equipment selection (routers, switches, access points) balancing performance, scalability, and budget for a multinational company operating across Mexico and the United States.
velatia.costBullet1: TCO (Total Cost of Ownership) analysis for equipment across 5 sites
velatia.costBullet2: Equipment selection balancing performance vs. corporate budget
velatia.costBullet3: Redundancy and high availability planning for critical links
velatia.costBullet4: Scalable design prepared for future company growth
velatia.securityDesc: Integral security and awareness component for the corporate network. Implementation of security best practices in network device configuration and development of digital hygiene training material for organization employees.
velatia.securityBullet1: Strong password policies and network credential management
velatia.securityBullet2: Banking and sensitive data protection in corporate communications
velatia.securityBullet3: Phishing awareness (SMS/email) and digital identity protection
velatia.certDesc: Diploma awarded by Velatia for creating the best network design in the class. The recognition validates the quality of the infrastructure design presented, highlighting the solution as the most complete and professional among all participating teams.
